description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels, which may serve both functional and aesthetic purposes. The block is solid and robust, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform, dark coloration, suggesting it is made from a dense stone such as granite or basalt. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. There are no visible signs of weathering, markings, or engravings, giving it a modern and utilitarian appearance. Its proportions and design indicate it could be used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a traffic control system.
description: A solid, rectangular stone block with a grooved, striped top surface and smooth, dark sides, likely made from dense stone. Its modern, clean design suggests use as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth of the grooves and the block's thickness, with grooves running away from the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern from the opposite side with grooves running horizontally.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the depth and spacing of the grooves, mirroring view 1.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
